english: rigs (rigs) gós-rigs (gos rigs) mi-¸drá-ba (mi 'dra ba) different languages and costumes; cì-rigs (ci rigs) of every sort, cì-rigs-su (ci rigs su) in every possible manner, e. g. čos (chos) stón-pa (ston pa) to teach religion; nyín-moí (nyin mo'i) rigs-kyis (rigs kyis) or rigs-la (rigs la) by the day, by days, daily Glr.; rigs (rigs) is also used for some, certain, nád-rigs-la (nad rigs la) mi (mi) ¸dzém-na ('dzem na) if one is not on his guard against certain diseases; sometimes pleon.: yán-lag (yan lag) rigs (rigs) bźi (bzhi) the four limbs, viz. hands and feet Glr.; rgyal-čén (rgyal chen) rigs (rigs) bźi (bzhi) the four great spirit-kings Thgy., rigs (rigs) gsum (gsum) mgón-po (mgon po) the three tutelar saints (spyan-ras-gzigs (spyan ras gzigs), p'yag-rdór (phyag rdor), ¸jam-dbyáńs ('jam dbyangs)) Glr.; sańs-rgyás (sangs rgyas) rigs (rigs) lńai (lnga'i) źiń-k'ams (zhing khams) Thgr. — rigs-pa (rigs pa) vb. to have the way, manner, custom, quality of, mgo (mgo) p'yir (phyir) ¸byúń-bai ('byung ba'i) rigs-so (rigs so) the upper end (of a stick, part of which is in the water) has the way of sticking out, i.e. sticks out; often to be trans-lated: must necessarily (according to the laws of nature or to circumstances); as partic. or adj.: necessary, also proper, suitable, right, suited to its purpose, in the earlier literature gen. with the genit. of the infin., sometimes with the termin. of the infin., in later times with the root of the verb; thus: t'ós-nas (thos nas) ldáń-bai (ldang ba'i) rigs-so (rigs so) you must get up as soon as you hear . . . Dzl.; da (da) ri (ri) źig (zhig) snáń-bai (snang ba'i) rigs (rigs) now a mountain must appear Dzl.; bźág-pai (bzhag pa'i) rigs-sam (rigs sam) would it not be expedient to appoint...? Dzl.; rigs-kyi (rigs kyi) dús-la (dus la) báb-bo (bab bo) it is just the right time Dzl.; mi (mi) smrá-bai (smra ba'i) mi (mi) rigs-so (rigs so) it is not right to be silent Dzl.; smád-par (smad par) mi (mi) rigs-so (rigs so) it is not right to abuse Glr.; ¸ón-rigs ('on rigs) ¸dúg-pas ('dug pas) because (he) might possibly come Mil. nt.; drán-pa (dran pa) mi (mi) zin (zin) rigs-la (rigs la) if he should perhaps not retain the recollection of, if there should be any danger of his not remem-bering Thgr.; ńan-sóń-du (ngan song du) ¸gró-bai ('gro ba'i) rigs-la (rigs la) as there is a possibility of going to hell Thgr.; gdúl-bai (gdul ba'i) rigs-pa (rigs pa) those fit for conversion Dzl.; lhar (lhar) skyé-ba (skye ba) ni (ni) rigs-pa (rigs pa) ma (ma) lags (lags) his being re-born as a deity is not befitting, or also: not possible, not probable Dzl.; mi-rigs-pa (mi rigs pa) wrong, not right, unbecoming, im-proper etc., mostly as adv.: mi-rigs-par (mi rigs par) byéd-pa (byed pa) to act wrong, to do badly, frq. — rigs-kyi (rigs kyi) rjes-¸bráń (rjes 'brang) Was. (274) v. sub luń (lung). Comp. rigs-brgyúd (rigs brgyud) race, lineage, extrac-tion, family Cs., rigs-brgyud-¸dzin (rigs brgyud 'dzin) male issue, rigs-brgyúd (rigs brgyud) ¸pél-bar ('phel bar) gyúr-ba (gyur ba) the rising of a numerous progeny Dom. — rigs-ńán (rigs ngan) 1. low birth or extraction, *kyod (khyod) mi (mi) rig-ńán-pa (rig ngan pa) dań (dang) nyám-po (nyam po) ¸dé-će ('dre ce) man* (man) you must not mingle with people of low extraction, with common people W.; rigs-ńán (rigs ngan) dpón-du (dpon du) skó-ba (sko ba) to raise a child of low extraction to the royal dignity Glr. 2. hangman Dzl. (cf. gdól-pa (gdol pa)). — rigs-can (rigs can), rigs-ldán (rigs ldan) of noble birth. — rigs-mnyám-pa (rigs mnyam pa), mt'ún-pa (mthun pa), ¸drá-ba ('dra ba) of the same rank etc., of the same species. — rigs-nyáms (rigs nyams) degenerated, rigs-nyáms (rigs nyams) dge-slóń (dge slong) a monk disgracing his profession Pth. — rigs-méd (rigs med) = rigs-ńán (rigs ngan) no. 1.

1. v. rigs (rigs). — 2. often erron. for rig-pa (rig pa). — 3. adj. of rigs (rigs): rgyál-rigs-pa (rgyal rigs pa) belonging to the reigning family or caste; cì-rigs-pai (ci rigs pa'i) sgó-nas (sgo nas) in every possible manner Mil.; cì-rigs-par (ci rigs par) snyán-pai (snyan pa'i) ts'íg-gis (tshig gis) with ever so many kind words Dzl.; also: in any way, any how, to a certain degree or extent, in part, partly Tar. 4, 3 etc. — 4. sbst., translation of न्याय logic, dialectics Trigl. 15; an infallible, not deceptive idea Was. (297).
continual (?), daily (?) ka-tsá (kha tsha) riń-ńe-ba (ring nge ba) Mil. nt. every day warm meals.

I. adj., also riń-po (ring po) C., B., *riń-mo* (ring mo) W. 1. long, high, tall, relating to space; riń-mo (ring mo) *k'ur* (khur) W. carry it lengthwise; it also implies distance, in which case t'ag-riń (thag ring) (q. v.) is the more precise form; da-dúń (da dung) yúl-las (yul las) riń-ste (ring ste) as he is still at a great dis-tance from the place Dzl.; more frq. with dań (dang): gnas (gnas) ¸di ('di) groń-k'yér (grong khyer) dań (dang) riń-bas (ring bas) because this place is far from the town Dzl.; mi (mi) riń-ba-na (ring ba na) at no great distance. — 2. long, with respect to time, ts'e (tshe) riń-ba (ring ba) sbst. a long life, adj. long-lived, rgyál-ba (rgyal ba) dań (dang) ts'e (tshe) riń-bar (ring bar) śog (shog) cìg (cig) may he be victorious and live long! Dzl.; yun (yun) riń-po (ring po) (or mo (mo)) a long time; yun (yun) riń-po-nas (ring po nas) from a long time, a long time since, riń-por (ring por) ma (ma) lón-par (lon par), less accurately: riń-po (ring po) ma (ma) lón-par (lon par) soon afterwards, relating to things past, riń-por (ring por) mi (mi) t'ógs-par (thogs par) id. with respect to the future, = after a little while, in a short
